titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Harold C. Conklin Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by staff of Manuscripts and Archives

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday July 27, 2019 at 07:36
finding aid revision description not supplied
finding aid revision description not supplied
finding aid revision description not supplied

did.abstract: The papers comprise correspondence, maps, and topical files documenting the research of anthropologist Harold C. Conklin and his colleagues, particularly in the area of linguistics and focusing on the Philippines. The papers also document Conklin's professorial, curatorial, and administrative work at Yale University.

langmaterial.language: The materials are primarily in English, with specific topical files containing materials in Ilocano and Tagalog.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the Estate of Harold C. Conklin, 2009, 2016, and 2018.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The bulk of the collection is open for research.

Access to Yale University administrative files is prohibited for 35 years from creation of the records. Access to student and personnel files is prohibited for 75 years or life plus five years (whichever is longer). Access to administrative, student, and personnel records is established by Yale Corporation regulations http://web.library.yale.edu/mssa/collections/research-use-of-yale-university-archives.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection whether such works are at Yale or physically located elsewhere in the world. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Harold C. Conklin Papers (MS 1956). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Harold C. Conklin Philippine Collection (AFC 2001/007), Archive of Folk Culture, American Folklife Center, Library of Congress.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Harold C. Conklin (1926-2016) received his BA degree from the University of California, Berkeley, in 1950 and his PhD from Yale University in 1955. He taught anthropology at Columbia University from 1954 until 1962, at which time he joined the Yale faculty. Conklin served as chair of the Department of Anthropology, curator of Anthropology, and director of Graduate Studies, as well as director of the Division of Anthropology at the Peabody Museum of Natural History. An emeritus professor, he retired from Yale in 1996. He was also the Frank Muzzy Crosby Professor Emeritus of the Human Environment. Conklin pioneered research in the field of ethnoscience, a discipline dealing with the way in which inhabitants of a particular area understand and treat their environment. His research focused primarily on ecology, ethnology, and linguistics in Southeast Asia, particularly the Philippines, and he authored numerous publications and articles in his field. Conklin died on February 18, 2016.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers comprise personal and professional correspondence, maps, and topical files, documenting the research of Conklin and his colleagues, particularly in the area of language in the Philippines. Maps were created in part by the American Geographical Society and the Army Map Service. The Topical Files include professional events and organizations in which Conklin participated; research materials of colleagues Leonard Bloomfield, Frederick Russell Eggan, and Robert B. Fox; and materials relating to the Tasaday Controversy, in which the Tasaday, an indigenous people of Mindanao, a Philippine island, were considered in the 1970s-1980s to be the last vestiges of a stone age culture. Anthropologists and journalists questioned the validity of this claim. Additional files document Conklin's professorial, curatorial, and administrative activities at Yale, particularly in the Department of Anthropology and the Peabody Museum of Natural History.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in three series and two additions: I. Correspondence, 1944-2005. II. Maps, 1908-1977. III. Topical Files, 1897-1998.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged as it was received from Harold Conklin, in rough chronological order based on meaningful periods in his career.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Maps deal primarily with land usage, district drainage, and related topics in the Philippine islands.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1977 enlargement of a 1908 Hodgson and Briesmeister map (1 : 2500,000; 1 : 1,115,000).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Labeled: AGS (American Geographical Society), underlay and overlay, plate 35-C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Labeled: Area B = NC 1, 8 in 1, 1 : 5,000, AGS.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Label: Area I aerial photo coverage, 1 : 50,000, paper underlay of combined AMS (Army Map Service, Corps of Engineers) sheets (hab + 1967), 1972; film overlay - air photo index; ISA/tg 1972, H's negs; PAF photography, 1963-1966.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 : 50,000 MS and AS sheets: (M-362) overlay, Plate 4; (M-362) Santos, 1969 Apr 16, Plate 4, AP 54; (M-363) color tint elevation band overlay, 1969 Jun 9,Plate 4; (M-364X) Plate 5, District drainage, Ifugao, 1974; Plate 5, 3 small maps of Ifugao districts (1973-1975).

did.unittitle: Pu'itan, Kinnakin, Bayninan (North Central Ifugao), Philippines

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 : 5,000, from AGS (American Geographical Society) 1972 maps.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 : 12,500, black and white, linear lined.

langmaterial.language: The materials are primarily in English. Some notecards contain Ilocano and Tagalog with English translations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topical Files include professional events and organizations in which Conklin participated; research materials of colleagues Leonard Bloomfield, Frederick Russell Eggan, and Robert B. Fox; and materials relating to the Tasaday Controversy. (The Tasaday, an indigenous people of Mindanao, a Philippine island, were considered in the 1970s-1980s to be the last vestige of a stone age culture. The validity of this claim was questioned by anthropologists and journalists.)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged by subject and alphabetically therein.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of 2"x5" note cards, some with Slavic forms on the verso.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes memorial brochure, HCC notes, and information on Eggan's papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession comprises Yale University administrative and teaching files, topical files, professional service files, and correspondence documenting Harold Conklin's professional responsibilities at Yale University and in professional organizations. The Yale University files document Harold Conklin's tenure at Yale University, including trips with the Association of Yale Alumni, teaching and research records from the Anthropology and Linguistics departments, and curatorial files from the Peabody Museum of Natural History. The records also document Conklin's professorial and administrative responsibilities at Yale, particularly in the Department of Anthropology, such as his chair and committee service, courses and exhibitions, the Agrarian Studies Program, Southeast Asia Studies activities, and the development of Human Relations Area Files. Those activities are represented through memoranda, meeting minutes, course and student files, personnel and financial records, event ephemera, and exhibition planning.

Topical files contain field research, correspondence, and biographical materials of Conklin's colleagues and students, including Patricia O. Afable, Richard F. Barton, Arthur Einhorn, Michiko Takaki, and Owen A. Tomlinson. Files on Floyd G. Lounsbury, William H. Scott, and William C. Sturtevant were compiled by Harold Conklin to write their obituaries.
Professional service files include records of conference activities, presentations, and projects in which Conklin participated.
Correspondence documenting Conklin's professional activities and communications with colleagues is present throughout the accession and is also included in the correspondence grouping.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The accession is arranged in four groupings: Yale University, Topical files, Professional service files, and Correspondence.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Manuscripts and Archives staff grouped material on similar subjects together during processing to facilitate access by researchers. Folders without titles and loose papers were titled during processing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping includes files documenting Harold Conklin's tenure at Yale University as a professor in the Department of Anthropology and as Curator of Anthropology for the Peabody Museum of Natural History. Materials include research on the history of the Department of Anthropology, Conklin's teaching files, meeting minutes, and notes from councils and study groups in which Conklin participated.
Files are arranged by subject in alphabetical order. Additional materials related to the Peabody Museum are also in the Professional service grouping.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The bulk of the materials is open for research. Yale University student, personnel, and administrative records are restricted as established by Yale Corporation regulations http://web.library.yale.edu/mssa/collections/research-use-of-yale-university-archives. Any restricted records are noted as such.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Filmed 1952-1954 by Conklin, originally released as a 16mm film in 1957 and re-released as a videotape in 1993.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The computer files are unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access should email mssa.assist@yale.edu . The request should outline the scope and purpose of the research project, why the researcher believes the material is relevant to their project, and contact information. If possible the request should also include a list of specific material of interest including collection, box, and folder numbers (or folder descriptions if folders are not numbered). The review may take several weeks.

Original born digital files, as well as preservation masters, may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist request that they be made. Born digital files cannot be accessed remotely. System requirements include a Manuscripts and Archives computer and file viewing software.

If possible the request should also include a list of specific material of interest including collection, box, and folder numbers (or folder descriptions if folders are not numbered). The review may take several weeks.

Original born digital files, as well as preservation masters, may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist request that they be made. Born digital files cannot be accessed remotely. System requirements include a Manuscripts and Archives computer and file viewing software.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files contain correspondence, notes, events, and information about study groups and research for Southeast Asia Studies at Yale University. Conklin was particularly active in the Council on Southeast Asia Studies and the Agrarian Studies program. Materials relating to Southeast Asia Studies conferences and meetings are also present in the Professional service files.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to administrative records is restricted until 1 January 2043 by Yale University policy. Contact Public Services for information on requesting access to restricted university records.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Agrarian Studies is an interdisciplinary program at Yale University, established in 1991. It awards Visiting Research Fellowships for scholars to conduct research in New Haven for an academic year and participate in the related colloquium series.

did.unittitle: Alumni, History PhDs in Southeast Asia Studies
Concilia on International Studies at Yale University, flyers and brochure
Conklin talk at Southeast Asia Studies (SEAS) reunion 1 March 2002

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping includes topical files Conklin maintained on his colleagues and students. Materials on Patricia O. Afable and Michiko Takaki, doctoral students who completed dissertations under Conklin, include personal and professional correspondence, field research notes, recommendation letters, writings, events, and advising on anthropological research. The Richard F. Barton files document Conklin's research on him and include Barton's original correspondence and records documenting projects and grants for his linguistic and film work in the Philippines. These files also contain Barton family correspondence and Barton's personal correspondence detailing his experience as a prisoner in the Philippines during World War I and transition back to the United States. Similarly, the Owen A. Tomlinson files include letters Tomlinson wrote to family, as well as photographs Tomlinson took while he was an officer in the Philippine Constabulary, Ifugao, Mountain Province. The Arthur Einhorn materials primarily include incoming and outgoing correspondence by Einhorn regarding anthropology topics.

Materials related to Floyd G. Lounsbury (Conklin's dissertation advisor at Yale University), William H. Scott, and William C. Sturtevant were collected by Conklin to write about their contributions to the field of anthropology for their obituaries. The materials contain writings and presentations by each individual, biographical notes by Conklin, correspondence, and printed material. Files on Sturtevant include information on his contribution to the use of ethno- as a technical prefix in cultural anthropology.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, articles, photographs, clippings, and reports documenting research in the Philippines.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The computer files are unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access should email mssa.assist@yale.edu . The request should outline the scope and purpose of the research project, why the researcher believes the material is relevant to their project, and contact information. If possible the request should also include a list of specific material of interest including collection, box, and folder numbers (or folder descriptions if folders are not numbered). The review may take several weeks.

Original born digital files, as well as preservation masters, may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist request that they be made. Born digital files cannot be accessed remotely. System requirements include a Manuscripts and Archives computer and file viewing software.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping comprises drafts, data, correspondence, and applications related to publishing a revision of the dissertation Michiko Takaki completed under the guidance of Harold Conklin, titled Exchange in Kalinga: Economic Relations in Traditional Society .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping includes files documenting Conklin's professional service and activities with organizations engaged in anthropological research. Materials include meeting notes, conference planning, annual reports, and membership information for the organizations.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: These files have been arranged in alphabetical order by organization name. Files within organizational groupings have maintained in the order in which they were received by the repository, possibly in Conklin's original filing order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping includes correspondence between Harold Conklin and his colleagues. Files with individual correspondents pertain to their work in the field of anthropology or personal matters.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The bulk of materials is open for research. Student records are restricted until 2085 as established by Yale Corporation regulations http://web.library.yale.edu/mssa/collections/research-use-of-yale-university-archives.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Files are arranged under two headings: Alphabetical files and Chronological files. The Alphabetical files are arranged by correspondent or subject as identified by Conklin or Conklin's assistant(s). Chronological files, which were received by the repository in rough chronological order, are arranged in chronological order.

did.unittitle: Beyer Collection in Philippine Archaeology and Ethnology

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The computer files are unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access should email mssa.assist@yale.edu . The request should outline the scope and purpose of the research project, why the researcher believes the material is relevant to their project, and contact information. If possible the request should also include a list of specific material of interest including collection, box, and folder numbers (or folder descriptions if folders are not numbered). The review may take several weeks.

Original born digital files, as well as preservation masters, may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist request that they be made. Born digital files cannot be accessed remotely. System requirements include a Manuscripts and Archives computer and file viewing software.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to administrative records is restricted until 1 January 2039 by Yale University policy. Contact Public Services for information on requesting access to restricted university records.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Includes student records, which are restricted until 2085 as established by Yale Corporation regulations http://web.library.yale.edu/mssa/collections/research-use-of-yale-university-archives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession comprises Harold Conklin's files from Yale University while he was a professor in the Department of Anthropology and the Department of Linguistics, and a curator for the Peabody Natural History Museum. The files document Conklin's administrative activities, events related to Southeast Asia studies, museum collections, and Conklin's correspondence with professional colleagues. The accession also includes student gradebooks and notes from courses Conklin taught at Columbia University and Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The bulk of the materials is open for research. Yale University student, personnel, and administrative records are restricted as established by Yale Corporation regulations http://web.library.yale.edu/mssa/collections/research-use-of-yale-university-archives. Any restricted records are noted as such.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to administrative records is restricted until 1 January 2047 by Yale University policy. Contact Public Services for information on requesting access to restricted university records.

did.unittitle: Native American community at Yale University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes newspaper clippings related to Skawenniio Barnes, Alyssa Mt. Pleasant, and current events.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Includes personnel records, which are restricted until 1 January 2087 as established by Yale Corporation regulations http://web.library.yale.edu/mssa/collections/research-use-of-yale-university-archives.

did.unittitle: Receipts and reports for research funding
Events and seminars in Agrarian and Southeast Asia Studies
Indo-Pacific Art at Yale University Art Gallery
Correspondence and notes on printing a textbook
Notes and news from Sterling Memorial Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters were compiled by Harold Conklin from his correspondence with Arthur Einhorn about their shared research on Northern New York Iroquois cultures. Files include articles, newsletters, and conference planning and handouts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This book contains student grades through 1996, then continues as Harold Conklin's personal notebook through 2008.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Student records are restricted until 2071 as established by Yale Corporation regulations http://web.library.yale.edu/mssa/collections/research-use-of-yale-university-archives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping comprises notecards that contain Harold Conklin's handwritten notes related to his work at Columbia University and Yale University. The files include notecards on individual people.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notecards on the Yale University Department of Anthropology, the Peabody Museum of Natural History, the Yale University Linguistics Department, and the Yale University Southeast Asian Studies Department.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notecards on student researchers, colleagues, events, and faculty of Yale University Department of Anthropology.
